details: Investigating device hang in SDPA Flash Attention compute kernel, case 1x1x32x32, stall at/after phase E (first BroadcastDim::Col op in eltwise_chain)

Read compute kernel, eltwise_chain.inl, bcast.h, reduce_helpers .inl, program descriptor, test. Reduce(non-matmul) calls reduce_uninit; phase E BinaryFpu Col-bcast init runs inside tile_regs window via sub_bcast_cols_init_short + exec sub_tiles_bcast<COL>. CB formats: cb_scores fp32, cb_max fp32, cb_scaler_max bf16.

DEVICE_PRINT markers: TR0(UNPACK) last=scaleQ done (NOT A done); TR1(MATH) last=A(QK) done (NOT C rowmax); TR2(PACK) last=boot (NOT scaleQ). Reader pushed Q + KV j=0. Stall is MUCH earlier than phase E -- at phase A (QK matmul) or phase C (reduce). Implementers claim phases A/C complete was misled by async prints.

Compute core (0,0) waypoints: BRISC=CWFW(writer waiting output), NCRISC=W(reader done), TR0/UNPACK=UPAD(finished llk_unpack_A -- copy/datacopy path, NOT matmul AB), TR1/MATH=MWDD(got DEST, computing), TR2/PACK=K(running, no LLK waypoint -- TRUE BLOCKER). Stall is at phase0->phaseA transition. Looks like DEST-sync deadlock not CB deadlock.

Phase 0 scaleQ does in-place rewrite of cb_q: CopyTile WaitAndPop (pop 1) + PackTile PerTileReserveAndPush (reserve 1, push 1). cb_q sized Dt=1 page. In-place CB rewrite with single-buffered CB: pop happens AFTER reserve_back in chain ordering? Need to verify reserve/pop ordering -- reserve_back(1) on a 1-page CB while the 1 tile still fronted (not yet popped) would block forever. Investigate chain pack vs pop ordering.

Per-thread markers: PACK never prints scaleQ done PACK -> PACK stuck in phase-0 eltwise_chain pack. MATH finished phase A, UNPACK mid phase A. PACK waypoint K (no LLK). PACK stuck in phase-0 pack step. Suspect: phase0 PackTile to cb_q(bf16) but packer hw_configured for cb_scores(fp32) at boot; PackTile default reconfig may not emit pack_reconfig_data_format -> pack stuck/corrupt. OR tile_regs_wait dest-sync deadlock.

In eltwise_chain per-tile path (always, since chain_is_hoist_safe=false), PackTile emit_pre_element_transitions (pack_reconfig_data_format) is NEVER emitted: elem_apply_compute no-ops pack-tile ops, hoisted_init_for_each only runs when hoist-safe. So packer stays at boot format (cb_scores fp32) when phase0 packs to cb_q (bf16). Need to verify this is the stall mechanism for PACK.

Adding explicit pack_reconfig_data_format(cb_q) before phase0 chain unblocked PACK from phase0 (now prints scaleQ done PACK + preA PACK). CONFIRMS missing PackTile pack-reconfig in eltwise_chain per-tile path was blocking PACK in phase0. Still hangs: now stall in phase A matmul (UNPACK+PACK at preA, MATH finished A). Second issue or moved stall.

DECISIVE: skipping phase-0 eltwise_chain entirely lets kernel sail through phases A,C,E,F (all threads). With chain present (even +explicit pack reconfig) it hangs in phase 0/A. CONCLUSION: the phase-0 eltwise_chain corrupts compute pipeline state. The implementer prime suspect (phase E bcast init) is WRONG -- phase E works fine without the chain. New waypoint UABD (unpack AB done) shows bcast path works. Hang now moves to later phase (F->H), separate/same-chain issue. Need to find WHY chain corrupts state beyond pack-reconfig.

Chain corrupts matmul state; phase A InitMode::Short does not fully restore. Production calls _uninit after eltwise; eltwise_chain calls no uninit. Candidate fix: re-issue mm_init after phase-0 chain before KV loop, OR restructure phase0. Testing: phase0 chain (with pack reconfig) + mm_init before KV loop.

copy+pack-only chain (no SFPU) STILL hangs phase0->phaseA identically. ROOT CAUSE: matmul_block InitMode::Short does NOT fully restore matmul hw state (half-sync DEST/pack-sync) after an eltwise_chain. Manifests at EVERY chain->matmul transition: phase0(chain)->A(mm) hangs; with phase0 removed, phaseE(chain)->F(mm-reduce)->H(mm) hangs at H. mm_init mid-MAIN makes it worse (undefined). Not SFPU-specific, not the phase-E bcast init (implementer suspect wrong).

Bug #2: matmul_block InitMode::Short uses mm_block_init_short which OMITS llk_math_pack_sync_init + llk_pack_dest_init + llk_pack_hw_configure. After an eltwise_chain these pack-side/DEST-sync states are eltwise-configured; Short does not restore them -> hang. op_design.md specified mm_block_init (FULL, has pack sync); implementer deviated to InitMode::Short. Helper InitMode enum has no Full mode. Fix: explicit mm_block_init() before matmul + InitMode::None. Testing.

Full mm_block_init (pack-sync re-init) + InitMode::None STILL hangs phase A. Bug #2 is NOT the missing pack-sync. Residual: after eltwise_chain, matmul_block(InitMode any) hangs with UNPACK=UPAD, MATH finishes (A done MATH), PACK stuck K. The chain leaves a deeper state issue (likely DEST bank parity / unpacker datacopy MOP not cleared, or an unbalanced dest-sync semaphore from the chain). Reverting experiment 5; keeping confirmed helper pack-reconfig fix.

Bug #1 (FIXED, HIGH confidence): eltwise_chain PackTile pack-reconfig never emitted in per-tile path -> phase-0 scale-Q packs bf16 into cb_q while packer set to cb_scores fp32 -> page overrun + DEST desync -> PACK hangs in phase 0. Fix: emit_pre_element_transitions in elem_apply_pack for PackTile. Confirmed: advances PACK past phase 0. Bug #2 (OPEN): matmul_block immediately after an eltwise_chain still hangs (UPAD/MWDD/K); full mm_block_init pack-sync re-init did NOT fix it. reduce-with-matmul survives a preceding chain but matmul_block does not -> deeper chain-exit DEST/datacopy-MOP state. Implementer prime suspect (phase E bcast init) is WRONG -- phase E works once phase 0 chain removed. Removed all DEVICE_PRINT from kernel; kept debug test.

Narrow task: find MINIMAL in-kernel reset between eltwise_chain and matmul_block that clears the hang, OR prove raw-LLK eltwise required. Bug#1 (PackTile reconfig) already fixed. Building minimal 2-op bisection harness on (1,1,32,32).

Built minimal bisection harness: real compute kernel reduced to mm_init boot + phase-0 eltwise_chain (scale Q) + phase-A matmul_block (QK), per-thread DEVICE_PRINT around matmul, then cb_out tile so writer drains. RESET_VARIANT macro selects reset between chain and matmul. Running variant 0 (control, expect HANG).

Harness control (no reset) HANGS, reproduces bug exactly. Waypoints UPAD/D/K. DEVICE_PRINT: UNPACK reaches pre-A not A-done; MATH reaches A-done; PACK reaches pre-A not A-done (stuck waypoint K=blocker). MATH completes matmul, UNPACK+PACK stuck.

reduce_uninit<true>(cb_scores) (what reduce does at exit) does NOT clear the hang. Identical signature UPAD/D/K, only MATH reaches A-done. On Blackhole _llk_math_reduce_uninit_<true> only clears a dbg bit; reduce->matmul works because reduce internally uses matmul (leaves unpack/math in AB mode), not because of uninit.

Variant 3 (explicit reconfig_data_format+mm_block_init_short, InitMode None) HANGS identically. Variant 5 (FULL mm_init mid-kernel) ALSO HANGS identically (UPAD/D/K, only MATH reaches A done). Decisive: NO matmul-side re-init/reset fixes it. mm_init re-inits all 3 threads but hang persists -> not a state-restore problem. Issue is a DEST/pack-sync handshake PARITY the chain leaves that re-init cannot undo mid-stream.

Instrumented matmul_block helper: PACK passes reserve_out, tile_regs_wait (mm:after tile_regs_wait prints), then HANGS in pack_reconfig_data_format(cb_scores) (mm:before pack_reconfig prints, mm:after never). Hang = TTI_STALLWAIT(STALL_CFG, PACK|THCON) in reconfig_packer_data_format (cpack_common.h:514). The pack_reconfig STALLWAIT after tile_regs_wait never clears -> packer has a pending DEST read from the matmul that does not complete after a chain.

v2 copy_tile_init, v4 no-op tile_regs cycle, v6 pack_reconfig-after-chain(idle packer-OK,no hang there), v7 llk_pack_dest_init ALL hang at the SAME matmul-internal pack_reconfig STALLWAIT. v6 proves pack_reconfig is fine when packer idle right after chain, but hangs after the matmul tile_regs_wait. So the broken thing is the matmul DEST handoff (MATH commit->PACK wait) after a chain, surfacing at the next pack config STALLWAIT.

DECISIVE: with fp32_dest_acc_en=False the chain->matmul transition WORKS (PACK reaches mm:packed/mm:released/P:A done). The hang is SPECIFICALLY the matmul_block internal pack_reconfig_data_format (gated on packer_l1_acc||fp32_dest_acc). With fp32 off that reconfig is skipped -> no STALLWAIT hang. Root cause: after eltwise_chain, the matmul pack_reconfig TTI_STALLWAIT(PACK|THCON) never drains in fp32 DEST mode. v6 (pre-issue same-format reconfig) did not help because matmul re-issues unconditionally.

v6(pre-reconfig after chain)+MM_SKIP_PACK_RECONFIG: matmul now PASSES (P:A done through mm:released). But harness trailing output chain (cb_scores->cb_out) THEN hangs at ITS pack_reconfig (matmul->chain transition, fp32->bf16). So the pack_reconfig STALLWAIT hang is BIDIRECTIONAL: fires at any op-boundary where a format-changing pack_reconfig follows a different-op pack in fp32 mode. Confirms the STALLWAIT(PACK|THCON) is the universal blocker, surfacing wherever pack_reconfig_data_format runs after a foreign-op pack.

VERIFIED: moving matmul_block pack_reconfig_data_format to BEFORE tile_regs_acquire/tile_regs_wait (issued while packer is idle, after the chains pack) CLEARS the chain->matmul hang. PACK reaches mm:early reconfig done -> packed -> released -> P:A done. The post-tile_regs_wait position hangs because tile_regs_wait sets up a fp32 DEST read that the reconfig STALLWAIT blocks on. ASYMMETRY: pack_reconfig after a CHAIN pack = OK; after a MATMUL pack = hangs even when idle (matmul pack poisons next reconfig). So real kernel matmul->chain (H->I) boundary needs separate handling, but chain->matmul (0->A) is fixed by early-reconfig.

PROBE confirms: bare pack_reconfig_data_format issued immediately after matmul pack (packer idle) HANGS the STALLWAIT. After a chain pack it does NOT. ROOT: matmul_block packs with pack_tile<true> (out_of_order_output=true) which leaves the packer address-counter in a state that the next pack_reconfig STALLWAIT(PACK|THCON) blocks on, in fp32 DEST mode on Blackhole. Chain packs with pack_tile<false>. The chain->matmul hang is fixed by issuing the matmuls OWN pack_reconfig BEFORE tile_regs_acquire (early, packer truly idle). matmul->eltwise is a separate downstream issue (out of this deliverables scope: eltwise_chain->matmul_block).
